summary: Second-Opinion is an impressively ambitious medical AI consultation app built with React 19, Firebase, and Gemini. It features a robust multi-step workflow (landing → auth → intake → upload → analysis → results), 43 components, 30 services, Firebase Cloud Functions backend, i18n (en/es), FHIR R4 export, AES-GCM encryption, PWA support, and a comprehensive test suite (23 test files). The architecture is clean with custom hooks, lazy-loaded routes, and proper separation of concerns. However, it's a Kaggle competition/prototype — not yet battle-tested in production with real patients. The sheer breadth of features (telemedicine, insurance pre-auth, clinical trials, research mode, provider dashboard) suggests many are thin implementations rather than production-grade. Coverage thresholds are set low (20% lines) which is honest but concerning for medical software.
strengths: Extraordinary feature breadth: 43 components covering telemedicine, research, glossary, admin, provider dashboards, timeline, insurance pre-auth, clinical trial matching
Clean architecture: custom hooks (useAnalysisPipeline, useAuthLifecycle, useStepNavigation), lazy-loaded routes, proper TypeScript enums and interfaces
Security-conscious: AES-GCM encryption for browser storage, PBKDF2 key derivation, server-side API keys via Cloud Functions, data retention cleanup
Healthcare standards: FHIR R4 Bundle export, medical disclaimers, uncertainty quantification, ensemble voting, citation tracking
Internationalization with i18next (English + Spanish), RTL support utilities
Full backend: Firebase Cloud Functions with Gemini integration, specialist matching, admin analytics, audit logging, SSE streaming
Testing infrastructure: vitest + testing-library + playwright, 23 test files covering components, hooks, services, integration
PWA with service worker, offline page, and manifest
Demo mode toggle for showcasing without real data
Zod validation, error boundaries, proper loading states
weaknesses: Coverage thresholds at 20% lines/statements — far too low for medical software handling patient data
Many features appear thin/aspirational: insurance pre-auth, telemedicine booking, provider verification, secure messaging likely aren't fully wired to real backends
No CI/CD pipeline visible (no .github/workflows, no Dockerfile)
No HIPAA compliance documentation or BAA references despite handling medical data
App.tsx is a monolithic orchestrator — could benefit from a proper router (react-router)
ErrorBoundary is .jsx while everything else is .tsx — inconsistency
No rate limiting or abuse prevention on the client side
functions/node_modules committed to repo (should be in .gitignore)
No accessibility audit evidence (aria labels, keyboard navigation, screen reader testing)
Version 0.1.0 — honestly labeled as early stage
recommendations: Add a proper routing solution (react-router or TanStack Router) instead of step-based state machine in App.tsx
Implement CI/CD with GitHub Actions: lint, type-check, test, build, deploy
Raise test coverage thresholds significantly — target 60%+ for medical software
Add HIPAA compliance documentation and audit trail for data access
Trim aspirational features and deepen the core flow (intake → upload → analysis → results)
Add proper accessibility testing (axe-core, keyboard nav testing)
Add rate limiting and input sanitization on Cloud Functions
Consider adding Sentry or similar error monitoring for production
Add E2E tests with Playwright for the critical happy path
